info.title: Lupira Contact API
info.description: Contacts, address books, and kinship backend for Lupira. Authenticate with a Bearer token issued by the OIDC provider (Authentik).

get.summary: The caller's resolved local identity (JIT-provisioned on first login).

post.summary: Idempotently ensure the caller has a personal address book; returns all accessible books.

put.summary: Link the caller's identity to its own contact ("this card is me") — the default focus for contact circles.

get.summary: List the address books the caller can access.

post.summary: Create an address book; the caller becomes its owner.

put.summary: Rename an address book or change its display name (owner only; merge — omitted fields are kept).

delete.summary: Delete an empty address book (owner only). 409 if it still holds contacts or groups, or is the personal book.

get.summary: List who has access to an address book and at what level (owner only).

post.summary: Grant a member access to an address book (access = owner|read-write|read; default owner).

delete.summary: Revoke a member's access to an address book (by email). 409 if it would remove the last owner.

put.summary: Update a contact (merge — provided fields overwrite/append, unmentioned fields are kept).

get.summary: Computed social circles (close family, extended family, friends, colleagues, household) around a focus contact — the caller's own linked contact unless focusId overrides. Degree is a closeness bucket (1 immediate, 2 two-generation kin, 3 cousin). Ended relations are excluded.

put.summary: Mark a contact as deceased (idempotent; the date may be unknown). Deceased contacts stay in the kinship graph — death is not deletion.

delete.summary: Undo a deceased marking recorded in error. (CardDAV can set but never clear deceased — clearing is API-only.)

put.summary: Replace the contact's social/IM handles wholesale. Service names are canonicalized; well-known services (telegram, messenger, whatsapp…) get the profile URL derived from the handle. At most one preferred handle per service.
Replace the contact's postal addresses wholesale; each entry needs a geo place id or a formatted address.
Replace the contact's emergency-contact designation wholesale (order = priority, empty clears). A designation, not a relation kind — your emergency contact is usually also a spouse or friend.

Replace the contact's reach channels (emails + phones) wholesale (empty clears). Unlike the merge update, this can remove a channel; values are trimmed, type tokens lowercased, duplicates dropped, at most one preferred per medium.
Replace the contact's tags wholesale (empty clears). Unlike the merge update, this can remove a tag; entries are trimmed and de-duplicated case-insensitively.

get.summary: Resolved relations, both directions: each entry's kind is the other contact's role relative to this one (incoming = derived inverse). Set includeInferred=true to also return kin derived from the parent/child graph (siblings, grandparents/-children, aunts/uncles, cousins, nieces/nephews), tagged Provenance=Inferred.

post.summary: Upsert a relation: 'toContactId is this contact's kind' (re-adding the same target+kind revises the label).

delete.summary: Remove the relation edge to a contact with the given kind — for edges entered by mistake. A relationship that ran its course should be ended instead.

post.summary: Mark a relation as ended (ex-spouse, falling-out): the edge stays, flagged with an optional end date, and no longer asserts current kinship. Re-adding the same relation revives it.

get.summary: List groups (personal groupings + organizations) in an address book.

post.summary: Create a group. kind = group|organization (an employer is an organization-kind group).
Add a contact to a group.

delete.summary: Remove a contact from a group.

Bearer.description: OIDC bearer token. Send as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.
